symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual, painless, bilateral blurred vision over two years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Slow painless bilateral blurring is the hallmark of cataract.
A chronic, gradual course argues strongly against a sudden vascular event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Disabling glare sensitivity and halos around bright lights, worse at night

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Light scatter through the opacified lens produces glare and halos, characteristically disabling at night.
Haloes can also occur in acute glaucoma — which is why pain/nausea/red-eye screening is required to separate them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Faded colour perception with yellowish tint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nuclear sclerosis yellows the lens and shifts colour perception.
A cataract-driven myopic index shift causes frequent refraction changes that do not restore function.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of contrast sensitivity — cannot distinguish stair edges in low light

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced contrast sensitivity is a functionally dominant cataract deficit and the direct mechanism of the near-miss fall.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-miss fall on internal stairs / high environmental falls risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult traumatic injury from the near-miss on the stairs
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any fall/near-fall in a frail older adult flags occult fracture/head-injury risk and mandates a falls-safety response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of ocular pain, headache, nausea/vomiting, red eye

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of the painful red-eye constellation excludes acute angle-closure glaucoma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of central scotoma or straight-line distortion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global glare-predominant loss without central distortion argues against macular degeneration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of sudden curtain-like monocular loss, flashes/floaters, or focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No sudden monocular curtain loss and no focal deficit excludes an acute vascular/retinal-detachment emergency on history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral cloudy/opaque lens on penlight inspection

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cataract cannot be confirmed or staged; a contributing retinal cause could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visibly opacified lens is the defining sign of cataract — SEALED clinician finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No signs of blunt trauma, fracture or focal neurological deficit on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult traumatic injury from the near-miss on the stairs
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person MSK/neuro examination excluded occult injury — a step the AI Doctor cannot perform, hence the need for prompt in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs within normal limits (e.g. BP 135/82, HR 88, afebrile, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal clinician-measured vitals argue against acute systemic deterioration; the home BP a neighbour read aloud is patient-reported and unverified, not a clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Formal visual acuity and slit-lamp cataract staging

investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced acuity with gradeable bilateral lens opacity
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cataract severity and surgical candidacy cannot be established; a contributing retinal/optic cause could be overlooked.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal in-person assessment confirms and stages the cataract and excludes a co-existing retinal cause — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Gradual painless blur + disabling glare/halos + yellow tint + ineffective refractions + contrast loss together strongly point to cataract.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Optometry review with visual acuity and slit-lamp examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and stages the lens opacity and excludes a contributing retinal/optic cause — the in-person step the AI Doctor cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ophthalmology referral for cataract surgical assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive management is phacoemulsification with IOL when visual impairment threatens function/safety such as driving and falls.
Addresses the dangerous element — the environmental falls risk in a frail older adult living alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Neuroimaging / carotid or vascular work-up for the visual change
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a chronic, bilateral, painless, glare-predominant visual decline once the acute vascular/neurological mimics are excluded on history — over-investigation of a red herring.
